skill.name: color-font-skill
skill.description: Choose presentation-ready color palettes and font pairings for PPT/design tasks. Use when users ask for visual theme choices, brand-safe palettes, or font recommendations. Triggers include: 配色, 色板, 字体, color palette, font, PPT配色, 字体搭配.
skill.summary: Provides curated color palettes and font pairings optimized for PowerPoint and design presentations.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: All 171 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner misinterpreted markdown backtick syntax (used for inline code formatting of hex color codes like #006d77) as Ruby/shell command execution. The C2 keyword and weak crypto alerts are also false positives triggered by color names and descriptive text in markdown tables. The file contains only static reference data - no executable code, no network calls, no file I/O, and no external command invocation. Safe to publish.

content.user_title: Add color palettes and font pairings to presentations
content.value_statement: Choosing colors and fonts for presentations takes time and often results in inconsistent designs. This skill provides curated, brand-safe color palettes and font pairings optimized for PowerPoint slides.
actual_capabilities: Provide 18 curated color palettes with hex codes for different themes and use cases
Recommend font pairings for Chinese and English text in presentations
Supply complete design system tokens including white, gold, blue, and gray color scales
Suggest color usage by element type (headings, backgrounds, borders, text)
Offer opacity variants for overlay, tooltip, and modal effects
Match color palettes to specific presentation scenarios (business reports, tech launches, weddings)
limitations: Only provides static color and font reference data - cannot generate or modify actual PPT files
Font recommendations limited to system-available fonts (Microsoft YaHei, Arial, Georgia, Calibri, Cambria, Trebuchet MS)
Does not validate color contrast ratios for accessibility compliance
Color suggestions are predefined - cannot generate custom palettes on demand

use_cases.title: Business presentation design
use_cases.description: A financial analyst uses the business and authority palette to create a professional annual report with consistent, trustworthy color choices.

use_cases.title: Brand-consistent marketing decks
use_cases.description: A marketing manager selects a matching palette from the 18 options to ensure brand consistency across campaign presentations.

use_cases.title: Academic and educational slides
use_cases.description: A university lecturer applies the education and charts palette to create clear, readable lecture slides with logical data visualization colors.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Get a quick color palette
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest a color palette for a technology product launch presentation with five colors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick palette request for a specific theme

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Font recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: What fonts should I use for a bilingual Chinese-English business presentation?

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Scenario-based palette
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a professional color scheme for an ESG report presentation. The palette should feel natural and trustworthy. Provide hex codes and usage guidance for each color.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed palette selection for a specific business context

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Full design system tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Provide the complete color token scale for the Platinum White-Gold theme, including all white, gold, blue, and gray tiers with their usage recommendations and opacity variants for overlays.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed design token reference for pixel-accurate slide design

output_examples.input: Give me a professional palette for a fintech pitch deck.
output: Platinum White-Gold: Primary #0A0A0A (text), #0070F3 (action), #D4AF37 (accent), #F5F5F5 (background), #FFFFFF (canvas). Use white-gold tones for premium feel, blue for calls to action, and near-black for body text.

output_examples.input: What fonts work for a Chinese product presentation?
output: Chinese: Microsoft YaHei (default). English: Arial or Georgia. Use YaHei for Chinese headings and body text, pair with Georgia for English headings and Calibri for English body text. System fonts ensure cross-platform compatibility.

best_practices: Use dark colors for headings and light colors for backgrounds to ensure readability in presentations
Limit palettes to five colors per presentation to maintain visual consistency and avoid clutter
Use opacity variants for overlays and backgrounds instead of solid colors to create visual depth
anti_patterns: Do not mix more than three distinct color palettes in a single presentation
Avoid using low-contrast color combinations like light text on light backgrounds
Do not embed custom fonts that may not render on other devices or presentation platforms

faq.question: How many color palettes does this skill provide?
faq.answer: The skill includes 18 curated color palettes, each with five hex color codes, usage scenarios, and styling recommendations.

faq.question: Can this skill generate custom color palettes?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides a fixed set of 18 predefined palettes. It serves as a reference guide, not a palette generator.

faq.question: Are the recommended fonts available on all operating systems?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends system fonts: Microsoft YaHei for Chinese and Arial, Georgia, Calibri, Cambria, and Trebuchet MS for English. These are widely available across Windows and macOS.

faq.question: Does this skill check color accessibility?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not validate color contrast ratios against WCAG standards. Users should verify accessibility compliance separately.

faq.question: What is the design system token section for?
faq.answer: The design system tokens provide a complete color scale for the Platinum White-Gold theme, including graded variants for white, gold, blue, and gray, plus opacity values for overlays and effects.

faq.question: Can I use these colors in tools other than PowerPoint?
faq.answer: Yes. The hex color codes are universal and can be used in any design tool, web development, or graphic design application.
